name: Vegetarian Roast Duck (Sue Sao Ya; Shanghai)
recipeInstructions:
The next few recipes are from a big, fat cookbook that would be several volumes if they had not used the somewhat unappealing format in which the recipes are presented. The book is pretty no frills, with little background given for the dishes. Almost everything that I've tried from the book has been good, though. Here we get into some hard-core, no-fooling Chinese vegetarian recipes. The book has a lot of vegetarian stuff in it, as well as recipes tailored for diabetics and those with ulcer problems. It's actually pretty easy to use once you get used to the odd format.
1. Wash mushrooms (B) and soak in warm water for 15 minutes; drain and save water; shred B very fine.
2. Mix light soy sauce (D), sugar, salt.
3. Mix mushroom water, cornstarch, sugar, light soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, mushroom water.
4. Fresh bean curd skin (N).
5. Peanut oil.
1. Heat peanut oil (A) and add mushrooms; stir-fry a few seconds; add shredded bamboo shoots (C); mix well.
2. Add mixed ingredients from step 2 to the pan; stir well and cook for 1 minute.
3. Dip pastry brush into mixed ingredients from step 3 and brush one piece of N generously.
4. Spread half of the A-G mixture on top; place second piece of N over this; brush again with mixed ingredients from step 3 and spread the remaining A-G mixture.
5. Repeat procedure until fourth layer is finished.
6. Fold into a 2-to-3-inch roll; place on a plate and steam over boiling water for 10 minutes; cool completely.
7. Heat peanut oil to 375F; deep fry assembled rolls until golden brown (1 to 2 minutes); cool. Slice and serve as hors d'oeuvres or serve with rice.
From "An Encyclopedia of Chinese Food and Cooking" by Wonona W. and Irving B. Chang, Helen W. and Austin H. Kutscher. Crown Publishers, Inc. New York. MCMLXX
